title: Printer Maintenance, Calibration, and Fault Isolation · Support Decision Defense
summary: A support panel compares near-miss cases for printer maintenance, calibration, and fault isolation and accepts only safe, authorized, causal, accessible, privacy-preserving, and independently defensible decisions. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for printer maintenance and causal repair record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Build a model-specific inspection, cleaning, calibration, consumable, counter, firmware, and replacement schedule from authoritative guidance.
Locate pickup, separation, feed, registration, duplex, exit, sensor, media, wear, and obstruction evidence before clearing a jam safely.
Use page position, repetition interval, color separation, density, missing regions, smearing, ghosting, and test pages to localize image defects.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# comptia-a-plus cumulative fictional support evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic text-contract review only. They never execute learner
# commands, inspect the host, touch devices or accounts, connect to networks, open equipment,
# handle malware, erase media, contact people, or claim certification.
workspace: support
ticket-id: fictional-ticket
user-impact: [replace with user outcome, scope, severity, and urgency]
environment: [replace with asset, version, configuration, owner, dependency, and evidence time]
safety-authority: [replace with consent, hazards, data custody, privacy, access, and stop rules]
baseline: [replace with expected behavior, known-good state, conditions, and measurement]
symptom: [replace with observed behavior, reproduction, frequency, timing, and evidence]
recent-change: [replace with timeline, relevant changes, counterevidence, and unknowns]
hypothesis: [replace with falsifiable cause, competing explanation, prediction, and confidence]
test: [replace with one safe reversible discriminating test, changed case, and stop rule]
observation: [replace with actual result, provenance, rejected inference, and next decision]
repair-rollback: [replace with least-change repair, authority, backup, trigger, and restored state]
verify: [replace with user outcome, original, changed, side-effect, restart, and recurrence checks]
prevention: [replace with prevention, monitoring, owner, review date, and residual risk]
escalation: [replace with stop threshold, destination, evidence packet, and requested decision]
documentation: [replace with plain structured status, redaction, access, handoff, and uncertainty]
transfer-boundary: authorized disposable equipment or simulator only
review:

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Repair Verification
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A technician replaces multiple printer parts before mapping jam position, repeated image interval, queue state, safe cleaning, or user outcome. The quiz variation 2 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Verify multiple representative pages, duplex and finishing, changed media, secure release, scan paths, counters, cleanup, and user outcome after repair.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Repair Verification.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for verify multiple representative pages, duplex and finishing, changed media, secure release, scan paths, counters, cleanup, and user outcome after repair. For CompTIA A+ support Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Repair Verification, keep Repair Verification connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: A technician replaces multiple printer parts before mapping jam position, repeated image interval, queue state, safe cleaning, or user outcome. stimulus.title: Repair Verification failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: One clean test page proves the complete multifunction workflow is restored.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Verify multiple representative pages, duplex and finishing, changed media, secure release, scan paths, counters, cleanup, and user outcome after repair.

options.text: Accept the result because visible output is the highest authority. That claim fails the evidence boundary for Repair Verification.

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Repair Verification
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Repair Verification: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A technician replaces multiple printer parts before mapping jam position, repeated image interval, queue state, safe cleaning, or user outcome. The quiz variation 4 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Verify multiple representative pages, duplex and finishing, changed media, secure release, scan paths, counters, cleanup, and user outcome after repair.
